title: Earthy Dense Venezuelan Tuna Chowder
description: A comforting, dense chowder brimming with earthy root vegetables, tender sousvade tuna, creamy brie, and a velvety sauce perfect for sickday comfort.

ingredients.name: Spices & acid
ingredients.notes: salt, pepper, thyme, bay leaf, lemon juice

instructions: Prepare the sous‑vide chamber. Vacuum seal the tuna fillet with a pinch of salt and pepper. Set the temperature to 125 °F (52 °C) and cook for 30 minutes.
While the tuna cooks, heat olive oil in a heavy pot over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add the diced carrots, sweet potato, and potato to the pot. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the vegetable stock, add the bay leaf, and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup until smooth, leaving some thickness.
Stir in the chopped kale and cook for an additional 2 minutes until wilted.
When the tuna is ready, remove it from the vacuum bag, pat dry, then slice into thin strips.
Add the sliced tuna to the pot and gently heat through.
Stir in the heavy cream, then add the brie cheese, allowing it to melt into the chowder.
Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, thyme, and a splash of lemon juice. Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs.
